---
title: 'Installation instructions' 
author: 'Fabio Molo and Gorka Fraga Gonzalez'
date: last-modified

---

To follow along in the hands-on part of the workshop, please install ```Quarto``` on your computer.  

## R and RStudio setup

Recent versions of RStudio have built-in support for ```Quarto```. Therefore, the simplest way to use it is to install recent versions of R (we recommend version 4.4) and RStudio by following the instructions at <https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/>.

To render documents in PDF format, ```Quarto``` needs to be able to access ```LaTeX```. If you do not already have ```LaTeX``` installed, you can install ```TinyTex``` by typing the following in your terminal:

```
quarto install tinytex
```

Additional instructions, if necessary, can be found here: <https://quarto.org/docs/output-formats/pdf-basics.html>


## Stand-alone installation

General instructions how to install ```Quarto``` independently of R RStudio can be found at <https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/>. In the course we will mostly use R and RStudio for demonstration, but ```Quarto``` can also be used with python instead of R and with VS Code, Jupyter Notebooks or other development environments instead of RStudio. If this is of interest to you, the following page is a good starting point: <https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/hello/vscode.html>

Please also make sure to have a working version of LaTeX installed (as above).
